findings.title: README boundary-disclaimer bullets migrated from CP00-153 section into CP00-154
findings.evidence: packages/audit/README.md: the new '## CP00-154 Review Sensitive Boundary Pack' heading is inserted before the two pre-existing generic bullets ('Hermes evidence rows ... descriptors only' and 'The catalog does not execute ...'). Those bullets previously documented CP00-153, so CP00-153's section is left with only its two function bullets, and the inherited 'PASS/PASS_WITH_FINDINGS/BLOCK closeout notes' wording describes CP00-153 closeout-verdict rows rather than CP00-154's row set (CP00-154 has a 'Go/no-go verdict format' descriptor, not closeout-verdict rows).
findings.recommended_action: Give CP00-154 its own boundary-disclaimer bullets and restore the disclaimer bullets under CP00-153; reword the inherited 'closeout notes' phrase to match CP00-154's go/no-go verdict-format descriptor. Cosmetic only.

findings.title: No-write assertions are tautological (validation theater), so a future real-write regression would not be caught
findings.evidence: packages/audit/src/review-sensitive-boundary-catalog.js freezeNoWriteResult() spreads the caller result then hard-overrides ~45 side-effect flags to false unconditionally; packages/audit/test/audit.test.js 'CP00-154 cases keep review-sensitive rows no-write' and scripts/validate-rp03-audit-architecture.mjs CP00-154 case loops then assert those same hardcoded false constants. The checks compare constants to constants rather than observing behavior.
findings.recommended_action: Accept as by-design for this descriptor-only/no-write pack — the module genuinely has no side effects and the contract↔code↔plan consistency checks are real. No change required for closeout; if regression protection is wanted later, add a guard that the module imports no ledger/fs/network surface.

findings.title: Coverage error paths and the unknown-case throw are not exercised by tests
findings.evidence: packages/audit/src/review-sensitive-boundary-catalog.js: validateAuditComplianceCp154Coverage() count/first/last/uniqueness/plan-order error branches and runAuditComplianceCp154ReviewSensitiveBoundaryCase()'s 'Unknown CP00-154 ... case' throw are only ever hit on the happy path; the added tests and validator call them only with valid inputs.
findings.recommended_action: Optional: add a negative test for an unknown caseId and a malformed planPack. Low risk; consistent with prior CP packs and not required for closeout.

parsed_review.p3_disposition: one fixed, two accepted as non-blocking descriptor-scope residual risk
